methodology: Cross-reference network (PSA.110.4→HEB.7.17=7v axis; ~3000-year typological arc Gn14→Ps110→Hb5-7) + patristic reading (Ephrem 'Melchizedek=Shem'; Cyprian 'bread/wine=Eucharist prefigured'; Chrysostom 'tithe proves superiority' + 'type vs reality: scriptural silence vs ontological eternity'; Aquinas 'without father=virgin birth, without mother=eternal generation'; Augustine 'priest by flesh, not by divine nature'; Haydock 5 proofs of superiority) + CCEL (Westcott 'aboriginal religion', Spurgeon 'majestic and sublime', Calvin 'chief father of the Church') + topic verification (MELCHIZEDEK, TYPES_OF_CHRIST)

evidence: commentaries (Ephrem 'this is Shem', Cyprian 'Eucharist prefigured — offered the SAME thing', Haydock 'non-bloody sacrifice', Theophilus, Ambrose)

new_refs_added.justification: Most theologically dense verse: 'Without father, without mother, without genealogy; without beginning of days or end of life, made like the Son of God.' 5v→JHN.1.1 and ISA.9.6. Aquinas: dual mystery (virgin birth + eternal generation + freedom from Levitical priesthood). Chrysostom: type ('we don't know because NOT WRITTEN') vs reality ('we don't know because it DOESN'T EXIST').
7v de PSA.110.4 (strongest connection in the network). 6v de HEB.5.6. Citation of Psalm 110:4 applied to Christ: 'You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.'

7v→HEB.4.14. 6v→HEB.5.6. Bridge verse: 'Jesus has entered as a forerunner on our behalf, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.' Connects priesthood to entry into the Holy of Holies (links to v2_ext_09 veil query).

candidates_noted.justification: 6v→PSA.110.4. Divine oath = irrevocability. 'The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind.'
5v de PSA.110.4. Prophetically confirms king-priest duality: 'He shall be a priest on his throne.'

reclassification.justification: The proper noun 'Melquisedeque' appears literally in every gold reference, making lexical retrieval trivial. 9/9 Gold-3 in enrichment confirms all sources agree. The real challenge is ranking: Hebrews 7 (theological exposition) should rank above Genesis 14 (brief narrative). Difficulty is in ranking precision, not retrieval.

four_theological_questions.description: 4 questions about Melchizedek answered by patristic commentators

questions.question: Who IS Melchizedek?
answers: Haydock: righteous Gentile, priest of true God
Ambrose: type of Christ ('Son of God')
Haydock REJECTS: not Shem, not the Son, not the Spirit

questions.question: Why superior to Aaron?
answers: Author of Hebrews: 'if Aaron's law sufficed, no need for another priesthood'

questions.question: What do bread and wine mean?
answers: Cyprian: 'prefiguration of Eucharist — offered the SAME thing'
Distinction: Melchizedek=bread/wine, Aaron=animal blood, Christ=body/blood in Eucharist

questions.question: Why 'without genealogy'?
answers: Aquinas: dual layer — virgin birth + eternal generation + freedom from Levitical tribal restriction
Chrysostom: type (scriptural silence) vs reality (ontological eternity)
Jerome: 'not that he had no father, but that it is not RECORDED'

typological_arc.significance: One of the longest typological arcs in Scripture. The ~1100-year silence between Psalm 110 and Hebrews is broken only by Christ.

chrysostom_type_vs_reality.distinction: TYPE (Melchizedek): 'We do not know his origin because it is NOT WRITTEN.' REALITY (Christ): 'We do not know his origin because it DOES NOT EXIST (he has no beginning).'
chrysostom_type_vs_reality.significance: Brilliant hermeneutical distinction: the same 'without genealogy' means something fundamentally different when applied to the type vs the antitype.
